current_columns.name: id
current_columns.notes: Primary key, stable IDs used for meeting_booking items

current_columns.name: content_type
current_columns.notes: De facto type field; no CHECK constraint in actual DB

current_columns.name: content
current_columns.notes: Stores body text (up to 10k chars per ingest)

current_columns.name: tags
current_columns.notes: Legacy flat tags field (JSON or comma-sep); normalized tags table also exists

normalized_tags_table.schema: (item_id TEXT FK, tag_key TEXT, tag_value TEXT, PK(item_id, tag_key, tag_value))

mismatches.actual_db: No constraint; content_type values: article, link, snippet, social-post, inspiration
mismatches.fix: Remove legacy constraint from _create_schema(); use content_type universally

mismatches.issue: ContentItem dataclass has 'type' field, DB has 'content_type'
mismatches.actual_db: Code has hybrid handling (line 133-134) but dataclass schema is misleading
mismatches.fix: Rename dataclass field to content_type; keep backward-compat alias

mismatches.issue: Duplicate path columns: source_file_path vs file_path
mismatches.actual_db: Both exist; source_file_path is used by content_ingest.py
mismatches.fix: Deprecate file_path, migrate any values to source_file_path

mismatches.issue: Tags stored in two places: items.tags TEXT and tags table
mismatches.actual_db: Calendly links use tags table; other items may use items.tags
mismatches.fix: Standardize on tags table for structured tags; items.tags for legacy/flat

new_columns_to_add.name: subtype
new_columns_to_add.rationale: Finer classification within content_type (e.g., content_type=link + subtype=calendly/scheduling/resource)

new_columns_to_add.name: summary
new_columns_to_add.rationale: Short AI-generated or curated summary (distinct from full content)

new_columns_to_add.name: managed_fields
new_columns_to_add.rationale: JSON array tracking which fields were set by automation vs curated by human; enables merge-safe enrichment

columns_to_deprecate.name: file_path
columns_to_deprecate.rationale: Redundant with source_file_path; migrate any existing values then ignore

curated_context_strategy.recommendation: Use tags table with reserved tag_keys

reserved_keys_for_scheduling_links.audience: JSON array stored as comma-sep values (one row per value)

curated_context_strategy.rationale: Tags table already exists with FK+index; avoids schema proliferation; flexible for future metadata

migration_plan.approach: Incremental ALTER TABLE + backfill; no destructive changes

sql: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN subtype TEXT DEFAULT NULL;
ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN summary TEXT DEFAULT NULL;
ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN managed_fields TEXT DEFAULT NULL;
steps.notes: SQLite ALTER TABLE ADD is idempotent if wrapped in try/except or checked

sql: UPDATE items SET subtype = 'calendly' WHERE id LIKE 'meeting_booking%' AND subtype IS NULL;
UPDATE items SET subtype = 'scheduling' WHERE content_type = 'link' AND (url LIKE '%calendly%' OR id LIKE '%meeting%') AND subtype IS NULL;
steps.notes: Conservative backfill; only sets if subtype is null

sql: UPDATE items SET source_file_path = file_path WHERE source_file_path IS NULL AND file_path IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_items_subtype ON items(subtype);

steps.action: Add composite index for common queries
sql: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_items_type_subtype ON items(content_type, subtype);

migration_plan.rollback_strategy: Columns can be dropped if needed; data preserved in tags table

content_library_py_changes.details: Add content_type (replacing type), subtype, summary, managed_fields; keep type as deprecated alias
Replace type CHECK with content_type TEXT NOT NULL
Map content_type, subtype, summary, managed_fields from row
Allow search(subtype='calendly') to find all scheduling links

backward_compatibility: Keep .type property on ContentItem as alias for content_type
Keep 'type' parameter in search() but map to content_type internally
Keep dict-style access (__getitem__, get()) on ContentItem

compat_risks.risk: Legacy callers expecting type='link' or type='snippet' only
compat_risks.mitigation: Alias type→content_type; most callers already handle multiple types
Document: tags table = structured KV pairs; items.tags = flat legacy storage
Make managed_fields optional; simple add() still works without tracking

notes_for_orchestrator: DB schema is already richer than content_library.py assumes — the legacy CHECK constraint exists only in the create_schema code, not the actual DB
Calendly/meeting links already have good tag metadata (audience, duration, context, purpose) via tags table — recommend keeping this pattern vs dedicated columns
The managed_fields column is key to the 'never overwrite curated context' invariant — it tracks which fields were set by automation vs human
W1.3 (ingest normalization) should ensure summary field is populated; W1.4 (Calendly sync) should set managed_fields appropriately
Consider adding a uniqueness constraint on (content_type, subtype, url) or (source_file_path) in future hardening pass, but defer to avoid breaking existing duplicates
Duplicate items exist (e.g., vrijen_work_30m_primary vs meeting_booking_vrijen_only_work_30m_primary) — W2.2 dedupe should address
